content:
Key Findings

  • Fylde Borough Council's spending increased by £15.9M (461.0%) from 2015 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £11.7M (104.9%) from 2017 to 2018
  • The largest year-over-year decrease was £10.3M (48.0%) from 2019 to 2020
  • Fylde Borough Council worked with 1,689 unique suppliers over 11 years
  • Spending in 2025 reached £19.4M, up from £3.5M in 2015

Spending Trends Over Time

Fylde Borough Council's spending has seen significant fluctuations over the past decade. In 2015, the council spent £3.5M, which surged to £10.5M in 2016, marking a 204.1% increase. This trend continued with another substantial rise of £11.7M (104.9%) from 2017 to 2018. However, the spending did not follow a consistent upward trajectory. For instance, there was a notable decrease of £10.3M (48.0%) from 2019 to 2020, bringing spending down to £11.2M.

The most recent data shows a steady increase, with spending reaching £19.4M in 2025. This represents a significant overall change of £15.9M (461.0%) from 2015 to 2025. The fluctuations in spending highlight the dynamic nature of the council's financial commitments and the varying demands on its resources.

Supplier Retention and Churn

With 1,689 unique suppliers over 11 years, Fylde Borough Council has a diverse range of vendors. The high number of suppliers suggests a strategy that may prioritise competition and variety in procurement. However, the data does not provide a clear picture of how many of these suppliers are long-term partners versus one-time vendors. Understanding the retention rate of suppliers could provide insights into the council's procurement efficiency and the stability of its supply chain.

To gain a better understanding of supplier retention, it would be beneficial to analyse the frequency and consistency of transactions with each supplier. For example, identifying suppliers that have consistently received contracts over multiple years could highlight reliable partners. Conversely, a high turnover of suppliers might indicate issues with contract performance or the council's procurement processes.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Fylde Borough Council about the retention rates of their top suppliers
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about procurement strategies and spending decisions
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers have received the most contracts over the years
  • Write to your local councillor about the importance of transparent and efficient procurement processes
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse trends in supplier transactions
This article is based on Fylde Borough Council's published spending data covering 2015/16 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
